key_insight: Active learning with uncertainty-guided sampling can identify sparse governing equations much more efficiently than random data collection in ultra-low-data regimes.
engineering_relevance: This enables cheaper and faster model discovery for physical systems when measurements are scarce, costly, or difficult to obtain.
method: Build on SINDy to recover sparse dynamical equations from data.
Use E-SINDy ensembles to estimate epistemic uncertainty.
Iteratively sample the most informative regions for ODEs and PDEs.
evidence_signal: The paper evaluates the approach on the Lorenz system across data budgets and noise levels, and on Burgers' and Kuramoto-Sivashinsky PDEs, showing accurate recovery with far fewer samples than random sampling.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of uncertainty estimates and on whether the informative regions are accessible for sampling in real experiments.
paper_title: How Low Can You Go? Active Learning for Sparse Model Discovery in the Ultra-Low-Data Limit
